title: Days after heatwave, French government cuts climate change adaptation funds
subhead: The Green Fund, which helps local authorities finance environmental protection and climate adaptation projects, is facing a €162.5 million cut, a nearly 20% reduction.

Zelensky's rehabilitation of controversial figures in Ukrainian nationalism sparks anger in Israel and Poland
subhead: By attending the reburial ceremony of Andriy Melnyk, a pro-independence leader who collaborated with Nazi Germany against the Soviet Union, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky is showing support for the country's evolving memory politics, which have been focused on opposition to Moscow since the February 2022 invasion.

title: PFAS: Six months after France bans 'forever chemicals,' the law is still not enforced
subhead: The Directorate General for Competition Policy, Consumer Affairs and Fraud Control (DGCCRF) is unable to carry out large-scale inspections to determine whether manufacturers are complying with the ban on per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS, also known as 'forever chemicals') in certain products, such as cosmetics and clothing.

title: The past, present and future cinema of director Céline Sciamma
subhead: From June 3 to 16, the MK2 Bibliothèque movie theater in Paris is hosting the first-ever retrospective dedicated to the filmmaker, featuring all her films, a masterclass and a conversation with actor Adèle Haenel.

title: The health of French presidents, a state secret
subhead: A new book unveils the last mysteries surrounding the illnesses of French presidents, especially that of Georges Pompidou (1969-1974), drawing on the diary of one of the president's physicians, Jean Bernard.

Bayeux Tapestry will be displayed flat for first time in London
subhead: The Bayeux Tapestry Experience is due to open to the public in the British Museum on September 10 and run until July 11 2027. It will be shown alongside other loans from UK and European institutions that will help put it into its medieval context.

title: The 20 French cities where the country's wealthiest live
subhead: The city of Neuilly-sur-Seine outside Paris has the highest proportion of very wealthy households in France, according to a study by the Inequality Observatory published on Tuesday. The ranking is dominated by the western suburbs of Paris and the areas surrounding Geneva.

title: The Fontainebleau Forest's ever-growing popularity threatens this jewel of biodiversity near Paris
subhead: Managers of the forest complex are wondering how to deal with the increasing influx of visitors: Will development contain overtourism or encourage it?
